enrichment.short_description: Corteza is an open-source, low-code platform (Apache 2.0) with a built-in CRM module for records-based business apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Corteza is a free, open-source low-code platform with a built-in CRM, messaging, and workflow automation, licensed under Apache 2.0.

features.title: Low-code CRM
features.description: Gives a 360-degree overview of leads and accounts, with a module for automating sales and service processes.

features.title: Unified Workspace
features.description: A Google Workspace-style collaboration layer bundled into the platform.

features.title: Enterprise Messaging
features.description: Built-in Slack-style internal messaging for teams.

features.title: Flattened RBAC
features.description: Role-based access control that lets organizations apply complex internal security policies.

features.title: Low-code app builder
features.description: Build and iterate custom records-based business apps beyond CRM.

features.title: Accessibility
features.description: The platform is built to conform to WCAG 2.1 accessibility guidelines.

pros: Fully open source under the Apache 2.0 license with no vendor lock-in
Code is contributed to the Commons Conservancy Software Foundation, so access cannot be revoked by a single vendor
Bundles CRM, internal messaging, and workspace tools in one self-hosted platform
Actively maintained on GitHub with recent commits and 2,000+ stars
cons: Requires self-hosting (e.g. via Docker) and technical setup to run
No first-party managed-cloud pricing page on cortezaproject.org; hosted instances are offered by third parties such as Planet Crust
Smaller community and ecosystem than major commercial CRMs like Salesforce

faqs.question: Is Corteza free?
faqs.answer: Yes. Corteza is fully open source under the Apache 2.0 license and free to download and self-host.

faqs.question: What license does Corteza use?
faqs.answer: Corteza is released under the Apache License 2.0.

faqs.question: Can I run Corteza without managing my own server?
faqs.answer: Corteza is designed to be self-hosted via Docker; hosted cloud instances are also offered by third parties such as Planet Crust, separate from the core open-source project.

faqs.question: Is Corteza only a CRM?
faqs.answer: No. Corteza is a broader low-code platform that includes a CRM module alongside a unified workspace and enterprise messaging.

use_cases.title: Open-source CRM without vendor lock-in
use_cases.description: Organizations that want a self-hosted alternative to closed-source CRMs and full control of their data.
use_cases.audience: IT teams and organizations avoiding vendor lock-in

use_cases.title: Custom low-code business apps
use_cases.description: Building bespoke records-based workflow apps on top of the same platform that powers the CRM.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Corteza CRM?
paragraphs: Corteza is an open-source, low-code platform that includes a CRM module for managing leads and accounts, alongside a unified workspace and enterprise messaging. It is released under the Apache 2.0 license, and its code has been contributed to the Commons Conservancy Software Foundation.
The platform is self-hosted, typically via Docker, and includes a flattened role-based access control (RBAC) system so organizations can apply their own internal security policies.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Corteza CRM is for
paragraphs: Corteza suits organizations that want an open-source, self-hosted CRM and are comfortable running and maintaining their own infrastructure, as well as developers who want to extend the same low-code platform to build other records-based business apps.

enrichment.short_description: Atomic CRM is an open-source CRM toolkit by Marmelab, built with React, Shadcn UI, and Supabase, that teams can self-host or customize.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Atomic CRM is an open-source CRM built with React and Supabase, offering free self-hosting or paid managed plans from €25/month.

features.title: Sales pipeline and Kanban board
features.description: Customizable Kanban board for tracking deals through a sales pipeline.

features.title: Contact and activity management
features.description: Contact organization with avatars, activity tracking, notes, and email-to-note capture.

features.title: Task management
features.description: Task creation with reminders tied to contacts and deals.

features.title: Data import/export
features.description: Import and export CRM data for migrations and reporting.

features.title: Single sign-on
features.description: SSO support via Google, Azure, Keycloak, or Auth0.

features.title: API and MCP Server
features.description: API integration plus an MCP Server for connecting AI assistants to CRM data.

features.title: Mobile app
features.description: A companion mobile app for accessing the CRM on the go.

pros: Fully open source with code available on GitHub, so any feature can be modified
No vendor lock-in — can be self-hosted on your own infrastructure at no cost
Built on a modern, well-documented stack (React, TypeScript, Shadcn UI, Supabase)
Includes SSO and an MCP Server for AI-assistant integration out of the box
cons: Paid Starter and Team plans cap contacts and storage (1,000 contacts/1GB and 10,000 contacts/10GB respectively)
Self-hosting requires managing your own Supabase/PostgreSQL infrastructure
Enterprise pricing is custom and not published

faqs.question: Is Atomic CRM free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it can be self-hosted for free since it is fully open source; Marmelab also offers paid managed plans starting at €25/month.

faqs.question: Who built Atomic CRM?
faqs.answer: Atomic CRM is built by Marmelab, using React, TypeScript, Shadcn UI, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Atomic CRM?
faqs.answer: Yes, self-hosting is supported for full data sovereignty, in addition to Marmelab's Supabase-managed hosting option.

faqs.question: Does Atomic CRM support single sign-on?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports SSO via Google, Azure, Keycloak, and Auth0.

use_cases.title: Customizable CRM without vendor lock-in
use_cases.description: Teams that want a CRM they can fully modify at the code level rather than adapting to a closed platform.

use_cases.title: Managed hosted CRM
use_cases.description: Teams that want the open-source toolkit without operating their own infrastructure, using Marmelab's Starter or Team plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Atomic CRM?
paragraphs: Atomic CRM is an open-source CRM toolkit from Marmelab, built with React, TypeScript, Shadcn UI, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase. It covers contact management, a Kanban-based sales pipeline, task reminders, and activity tracking.

Because the full source is available on GitHub, teams can modify any feature to fit their process instead of working around a closed vendor's roadmap. Marmelab also offers Supabase-managed hosting as a paid alternative to self-hosting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Atomic CRM is for
paragraphs: Atomic CRM fits teams that want to self-host a CRM and customize it at the code level, as well as smaller sales teams that prefer a managed hosted plan without giving up the option to self-host later.

enrichment.short_description: Krayin CRM is a free, open-source, self-hosted CRM built on Laravel, maintained by Webkul, covering sales, marketing, and support.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Krayin CRM is a free, open-source Laravel CRM for sales, marketing automation, and customer support, with 23,000+ GitHub stars.

features.title: Sales management
features.description: Lead tracking, deal management, and sales forecasting.

features.title: Marketing automation
features.description: Campaign management, email marketing, and customer segmentation.

features.title: Customer support
features.description: VoIP, live chat, and multi-channel feedback handling.

features.title: Reporting and analytics
features.description: Performance reporting and analysis tools for sales and support activity.

features.title: Custom fields and access control
features.description: Custom fields, activity logs, and access control lists for tailoring the CRM to a business.

features.title: Multi-channel communication
features.description: Connects with Gmail, Outlook, WhatsApp, Facebook, WeChat, Skype, and phone systems.

pros: Fully open source (MIT license) and free to self-host
Large, active GitHub project with 23,000+ stars and recent commits
Covers sales, marketing automation, and support in one CRM
Extensible with official extensions for VoIP, multi-tenant SaaS, and Google Workspace sync
cons: Self-hosting on Laravel/PHP requires setting up and maintaining your own server
No fixed first-party managed-cloud pricing; hosted setups are sold separately through Webkul's Cloudkul service and third-party hosts
Industry-specific solution templates and extensions add complexity to evaluate

faqs.question: Is Krayin CRM free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Krayin is fully open source and free to download and self-host.

faqs.question: Who maintains Krayin CRM?
faqs.answer: Krayin is maintained by Webkul as an open-source project.

faqs.question: What is Krayin built on?
faqs.answer: Krayin is built on Laravel (PHP).

faqs.question: Can I get Krayin as managed hosting?
faqs.answer: Webkul offers Cloudkul managed hosting setup on AWS, GCP, and Azure, and third-party hosts also offer Krayin hosting plans.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted sales CRM
use_cases.description: Tracking leads, deals, and forecasts without paying per-user SaaS fees.

use_cases.title: Support and marketing in one platform
use_cases.description: Combining live chat, VoIP, and email campaigns with CRM records.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Krayin CRM?
paragraphs: Krayin CRM is an open-source, self-hosted CRM built on Laravel and maintained by Webkul. It covers sales pipeline management, marketing automation, and multi-channel customer support (including VoIP and live chat) in a single self-hosted application.
The project has more than 23,000 GitHub stars and is actively developed, with extensions available for VoIP, multi-tenant SaaS deployments, and Google Workspace synchronization.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Krayin CRM is for
paragraphs: Krayin suits businesses that want a free, self-hosted CRM built on a familiar Laravel/PHP stack, and that are comfortable managing their own server or contracting managed hosting through Webkul or a third party.

enrichment.short_description: Odoo Community is the free, open-source (LGPLv3) edition of Odoo's business management suite, with 40+ integrated apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Odoo Community is the free, open-source edition of Odoo, offering 40+ integrated apps for accounting, CRM, inventory, and more.

features.title: Integrated business apps
features.description: 40+ apps spanning Accounting, Invoicing, Expenses, CRM, Sales, Point of Sale, Inventory, Manufacturing, Purchase, HR, and Website/eCommerce.

features.title: Open-source codebase
features.description: Source code is publicly available on GitHub under the LGPLv3 license.

features.title: PostgreSQL-based data model
features.description: Runs on PostgreSQL with no proprietary data format.

features.title: Community app marketplace
features.description: Access to 40,000+ community-developed apps.

features.title: Self-hosted or Odoo.sh hosting
features.description: Can be deployed on-premise or hosted via Odoo's own Odoo.sh cloud platform.

pros: Completely free to download, install, and modify under the LGPLv3 license
Broad coverage of business functions (finance, sales, inventory, HR) in one open-source suite
Large third-party app marketplace and active developer community
Backed by Odoo, a large, established company with 8,000+ employees
cons: Extra apps, infrastructure, and professional services are reserved for the paid Enterprise edition
Self-hosting requires managing your own server, or paying for Odoo.sh cloud hosting
40+ apps and heavy customization options can mean a steeper setup and configuration process

faqs.question: Is Odoo Community really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Odoo Community is open source and free to use, with no licensing cost.

faqs.question: What license does Odoo Community use?
faqs.answer: Odoo Community is licensed under LGPLv3.

faqs.question: What's the difference between Odoo Community and Enterprise?
faqs.answer: Enterprise adds extra apps, infrastructure, and professional services on top of the free Community edition.

faqs.question: Can Odoo Community be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, it can be self-hosted, or deployed via Odoo's own Odoo.sh cloud hosting platform.

use_cases.title: Free, self-hosted ERP core
use_cases.description: Running accounting, sales, and inventory on open-source software without licensing fees.

use_cases.title: Custom app development on Odoo's framework
use_cases.description: Building or installing community-developed apps on top of the Odoo framework.

features: Access to core apps and GitHub source

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Odoo Community?
paragraphs: Odoo Community is the free, open-source edition of Odoo's business management platform, licensed under LGPLv3. It includes 40+ integrated apps covering finance, sales, supply chain, HR, and website/eCommerce, all built on PostgreSQL.
It differs from Odoo Enterprise, which adds extra apps, infrastructure, and professional services on top of the same open-source core.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Odoo Community is for
paragraphs: Odoo Community fits businesses that want a free, self-hosted alternative to commercial ERP/CRM suites and are willing to manage their own deployment, as well as developers building on Odoo's open framework.

enrichment.short_description: Frappe Books is a free, open-source desktop accounting app for freelancers and small businesses that stores data locally in SQLite.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Frappe Books is a free, open-source offline desktop accounting app with double-entry bookkeeping and invoicing for small businesses.

features.title: Double-entry accounting
features.description: Records each transaction across two accounts for accurate financial tracking.

features.title: Financial dashboard
features.description: Overview of key financial data and performance metrics.

features.title: Point of Sale
features.description: Integrated POS for processing retail sales transactions.

features.title: Standard financial reports
features.description: Profit and Loss Statement, Trial Balance, and Balance Sheet reports.

features.title: Invoice template builder
features.description: A template builder for customizing invoice appearance using basic HTML.

features.title: Offline, local-first storage
features.description: Runs offline as a desktop app, storing all data in a local SQLite file for full data ownership.

pros: Completely free and open source with no subscription cost
Works fully offline, with data stored locally for privacy and data ownership
Cross-platform desktop app (Windows, macOS, Linux) via Electron
Data lives in a single SQLite file, making backups as simple as copying the file
cons: Desktop-only application, not a hosted web app by default
No official first-party managed cloud version dedicated to Frappe Books
Being a smaller project, it has fewer built-in integrations than larger accounting platforms

faqs.question: Is Frappe Books free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Frappe Books is free and open source with no subscription fee.

faqs.question: Does Frappe Books work offline?
faqs.answer: Yes, it runs locally and does not require an internet connection.

faqs.question: What database does Frappe Books use?
faqs.answer: It uses a local SQLite file to store all financial data and configuration.

faqs.question: What platforms does Frappe Books support?
faqs.answer: It runs as a desktop application via Electron on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

use_cases.title: Offline bookkeeping for freelancers
use_cases.description: Managing invoices, expenses, and reports without an internet connection or subscription.

use_cases.title: Privacy-focused accounting
use_cases.description: Keeping all financial data on a local machine rather than a third-party cloud.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Frappe Books?
paragraphs: Frappe Books is a free, open-source desktop accounting application for small and medium businesses. It supports double-entry bookkeeping, invoicing, a point-of-sale module, and standard reports like Profit and Loss and Balance Sheet.
Built with Electron, it runs offline on Windows, macOS, and Linux, storing all data in a local SQLite file so users retain full ownership of their financial data.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Frappe Books is for
paragraphs: Frappe Books fits freelancers and small business owners who want free, offline accounting software and prefer keeping their financial data on their own machine rather than in a third-party cloud.

enrichment.short_description: Akaunting is open-source accounting software for small businesses, free to self-host on-premise or available as paid cloud plans from $8/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Akaunting is open-source accounting software, free to self-host or available as cloud plans from $8/month, used by 300,000+ businesses.

features.title: Invoicing
features.description: Professional invoice templates with recurring invoices and billing.

features.title: Double-entry accounting
features.description: Chart of Accounts, Balance Sheet, and General Ledger.

features.title: Expense and cash flow tracking
features.description: Expense categorization and cash flow management tools.

features.title: Client portal
features.description: Lets clients view transactions and accept payments online.

features.title: Inventory and project tools
features.description: Inventory management, project tracking, and time tracking.

features.title: Payroll and CRM apps
features.description: Optional apps for payroll and basic CRM functionality.

pros: Self-hosted On-Premise Standard plan is free, with full source code available
Used by 300,000+ users across 100+ countries
Available in 50+ languages
Cloud plans scale from a single freelancer up to unlimited companies and users
cons: Higher-value features (payroll, inventory, CRM, POS) are gated to Elite/Ultimate cloud tiers
Cloud plan limits (e.g. invoice counts, number of apps) scale with price, so growing businesses may need to upgrade tiers
On-premise self-hosting requires managing your own server infrastructure

faqs.question: Is Akaunting free?
faqs.answer: The On-Premise/self-hosted Standard plan is free, with source code available; Akaunting also offers paid cloud plans starting at $8/month (billed yearly).

faqs.question: How many languages does Akaunting support?
faqs.answer: Akaunting is available in 50+ languages.

faqs.question: What's included in the cheapest cloud plan?
faqs.answer: The Standard Cloud plan supports 1 company, 1 user plus 1 accountant, 1,000 invoices, and 4 apps.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Akaunting?
faqs.answer: Yes, via the On-Premise version, separate from the hosted cloud plans.

use_cases.title: Free self-hosted bookkeeping
use_cases.description: Running invoicing and double-entry accounting on your own server at no license cost.

use_cases.title: Cloud accounting for growing teams
use_cases.description: Scaling from a single company to unlimited companies and users with add-on apps like payroll and inventory.
use_cases.audience: Small to mid-sized businesses and accountants

features: Double-Entry, Bank Feeds, Expense Claims, Client Portal
Payroll, Projects, Inventory, CRM, Shopify, Stripe
POS, budgeting, custom fields, tax regulation reports

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Akaunting?
paragraphs: Akaunting is accounting software for small businesses and freelancers, covering invoicing, expense tracking, double-entry bookkeeping, and a client portal. It's used by 300,000+ users across more than 100 countries and is available in 50+ languages.

The On-Premise Standard plan is free and open source, while Akaunting also sells hosted cloud plans (Standard, Premium, Elite, and Ultimate) that add features like payroll, inventory, CRM, and point of sale as you move up tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Akaunting is for
paragraphs: Akaunting suits freelancers and small businesses that want free, self-hosted bookkeeping, as well as growing businesses and accountants that prefer a managed cloud plan with add-on modules like payroll and inventory.

enrichment.short_description: Invoice Ninja is invoicing and business management software with a free plan, paid hosted plans from $14/month, and a self-hosted open-source version.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Invoice Ninja offers invoicing, payments, and time tracking with a free plan, paid plans from $14/month, and self-hosting via GitHub.

features.title: Invoicing
features.description: Customizable invoice templates (4 on the free plan, 11 on paid plans).

features.title: Online payments
features.description: Payment processing through multiple gateways including Stripe, PayPal, and Square.

features.title: Time tracking and projects
features.description: Time tracking and project management with Kanban boards.

features.title: Expense and vendor tracking
features.description: Expense tracking and vendor management.

features.title: Recurring billing
features.description: Recurring invoices and automatic billing.

features.title: Client portal
features.description: A client-facing portal for viewing invoices and making payments.

features.title: Quotes with e-signature
features.description: Quotes and proposal generation with e-signature support.

pros: Perpetually free plan available with 5 clients and unlimited invoicing
Open-source self-hosted version available via GitHub for full control
Wide range of native payment gateway integrations (Stripe, PayPal, Square, GoCardless) plus QuickBooks, Gmail, Slack, and Zapier/Make/Pabbly automation
Paid plans include a 30-day money-back guarantee
cons: Free plan limits invoice templates and client count
Higher-end e-invoicing/PEPPOL network access is limited to the Enterprise+ tier
Self-hosted version requires separate setup and maintenance versus the hosted plans

faqs.question: Is Invoice Ninja free?
faqs.answer: There's a perpetually free plan with 5 clients and unlimited invoicing, plus paid plans and a self-hosted open-source version.

faqs.question: How much do paid plans cost?
faqs.answer: Ninja Pro starts at $14/month ($140/year), and Enterprise starts at $18/month ($180/year) for up to 100 users.

faqs.question: Can Invoice Ninja be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, an open-source version is available for self-hosting via its GitHub repository.

faqs.question: What payment gateways does it support?
faqs.answer: It supports Stripe, PayPal, Square, GoCardless, and other gateways, plus integrations like QuickBooks and Zapier.

use_cases.title: Free invoicing for a handful of clients
use_cases.description: Sending unlimited invoices to up to 5 clients at no cost.

use_cases.title: Paid plans for growing teams
use_cases.description: Adding more clients, users, templates, and features like e-signatures and PEPPOL e-invoicing.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Invoice Ninja?
paragraphs: Invoice Ninja is invoicing and business management software for freelancers and small businesses, covering invoicing, online payments, time tracking, expense tracking, and a client portal.
It offers a perpetually free plan, several paid hosted tiers (Ninja Pro, Enterprise, Premium Business), and an open-source version that can be self-hosted from its GitHub repository.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Invoice Ninja is for
paragraphs: Invoice Ninja fits freelancers who want a free way to invoice a small client base, and small businesses or agencies that need more clients, templates, or e-invoicing/e-signature features on a paid or self-hosted plan.

name: Crater
skip_reason: Official website craterapp.com now permanently redirects to crater.financial, an unrelated AI-powered embedded fintech/AR-AP platform for developers, not the open-source self-hosted invoicing app for freelancers this record refers to. The original open-source GitHub repo (crater-invoice-inc/crater) still exists and is not archived, but has had no commits since August 2024 and no active official site describing it as a self-hosted invoicing product, indicating the maintainer has pivoted/rebranded away from it.

enrichment.short_description: Bigcapital is open-source accounting and inventory software (AGPL-3.0), free to self-host or available as hosted cloud plans from $20/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Bigcapital is open-source accounting software, free to self-host under AGPL-3.0 or available as hosted cloud plans from $20/month.

features.title: Sales and purchase invoicing
features.description: Invoicing with support for recurring invoices.

features.title: Financial reporting
features.description: Real-time financial reports for business intelligence.

features.title: Inventory tracking
features.description: Automatic stock management tied to sales and purchases.

features.title: Multi-currency accounting
features.description: Support for multiple currencies with live exchange rates.

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: Collaboration tools with custom user roles and access controls.

features.title: Budgeting and cost centers
features.description: Budgeting and cost-center analysis on higher plans.

pros: Open source under AGPL-3.0, so the full platform can be self-hosted for free
A commercial license option exists for organizations that need an AGPL exemption
Docker-based self-hosting is documented, with no per-user licensing fees
Hosted cloud plans include a 14-day free trial
cons: AGPL-3.0 requires releasing modifications if you deploy a modified version over a network
Multi-currency, inventory, and budgeting features are split across higher-priced plans (Essential, Plus, Big)
Company details like founding year and headquarters are not published

faqs.question: Is Bigcapital free?
faqs.answer: The self-hosted version is free and open source under AGPL-3.0; a managed cloud version is also available with paid plans starting at $20/month.

faqs.question: What license does Bigcapital use?
faqs.answer: Bigcapital is released under the GNU Affero General Public License v3.0 (AGPL-3.0), with a commercial license option available.

faqs.question: Can Bigcapital be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, self-hosting via Docker is documented and free.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for the cloud version?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bigcapital's hosted cloud plans include a 14-day free trial and a 30-day money-back guarantee on annual billing.

use_cases.title: Free self-hosted accounting
use_cases.description: Running accounting and inventory tracking on your own infrastructure with no license fee.

use_cases.title: Managed cloud accounting
use_cases.description: Using Bigcapital's hosted plans to get multi-currency accounting, inventory, and budgeting without self-hosting.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Bigcapital?
paragraphs: Bigcapital is open-source accounting and inventory software, released under AGPL-3.0, that automates invoicing, financial reporting, and stock tracking. It can be self-hosted for free via Docker, or run as a managed cloud service with paid plans from $20/month.
Paid plans add multi-currency support, inventory tracking, budgeting, cost-center analysis, and multi-branch support as you move up tiers, with a 14-day free trial available.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Bigcapital is for
paragraphs: Bigcapital fits businesses that want an open-source, self-hostable alternative to accounting platforms like QuickBooks or Xero, as well as those that would rather use Bigcapital's own hosted cloud plans.

enrichment.short_description: Firefly III is a free, open-source, self-hosted personal finance manager licensed under AGPLv3, with a full REST API.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Firefly III is free, open-source, self-hosted personal finance software with budgets, categories, rules, and a full REST API.

features.title: Double-entry bookkeeping
features.description: Tracks income and expenses using a double-entry bookkeeping system.

features.title: Budgets, categories, and tags
features.description: Organize transactions with budgets, categories, and tags to control spending.

features.title: Recurring transactions and rules
features.description: Create recurring transactions and rule-based automation for transaction handling.

features.title: Piggy banks
features.description: Save toward a goal using built-in piggy banks.

features.title: REST JSON API
features.description: A REST API that covers almost every part of Firefly III for integrations and automation.

features.title: Multi-currency support
features.description: Supports any currency.

features.title: Financial reports
features.description: Multiple built-in financial reports.

pros: Completely free and open source under AGPLv3, with no paid tiers or usage limits
Fully self-hosted and isolated — never contacts external servers unless explicitly configured to
Flexible deployment via Docker, Kubernetes, Softaculous, Cloudron, Lando, or Yunohost
Large, active project with 24,000+ GitHub stars and a documented REST API
cons: No official first-party hosted cloud version — users must self-host or use a third-party hosting provider
Requires technical setup (Docker or server administration) to run
As a self-hosted tool, backups and uptime are the user's own responsibility

faqs.question: Is Firefly III free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Firefly III is completely free and open source under the AGPLv3 license, with no paid tiers.

faqs.question: Does Firefly III offer a hosted cloud version?
faqs.answer: No official hosted cloud version is provided by the project itself; it is designed to be self-hosted, though third-party hosts offer Firefly III hosting.

faqs.question: Does Firefly III have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, it has a REST JSON API covering almost every part of the application.

faqs.question: How is Firefly III deployed?
faqs.answer: It supports Docker, Kubernetes, and installers like Softaculous, Cloudron, Lando, and Yunohost.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted personal budgeting
use_cases.description: Tracking personal income, expenses, and budgets without sending data to a third-party service.

use_cases.title: Automated finance tracking via API
use_cases.description: Building custom scripts or integrations against Firefly III's REST API to automate transaction entry.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Firefly III?
paragraphs: Firefly III is a free, open-source personal finance manager that is entirely self-hosted. It supports double-entry bookkeeping, budgets, categories, tags, recurring transactions, rule-based automation, and savings goals via piggy banks.
It is licensed under AGPLv3, includes a REST JSON API covering nearly every part of the app, and never contacts external servers unless explicitly configured to do so.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Firefly III is for
paragraphs: Firefly III fits individuals who want full control and privacy over their personal finance data and are comfortable self-hosting via Docker or a similar deployment method, including developers who want to automate finance tracking through its API.
